summary: I was born in Vietnam, and that’s where my life’s first chapter began. Growing up there, I learned many traditions and the rich history of my country. Leaving Vietnam in 2009 was difficult because I had to say goodbye to the culture I grew up with, but I carried it with me when I moved to America.

I grew up in Huế, which is also called the Imperial City. It’s a beautiful place where I learned a lot about my country’s traditions and history. Huế shaped much of who I am before I moved to America in 2009.

In 2009, I moved from Vietnam to the United States of America, which was a completely foreign country to me. It was hard to say goodbye to my culture, and at first, I struggled with fitting in because of my accent and background. Over time, especially after moving to Minnesota, I found communities that accepted me and helped me grow into a stronger person.

When I first came to America, I lived in California before moving to Minnesota. The transition was challenging, and I faced a lot of difficulties at school because of my culture and language. Moving from California to Minnesota marked a turning point where I began to feel more at home and accepted.

After leaving California, I moved to Minnesota, which became a place where I finally felt at home. I attended Humboldt Senior High School there and was surrounded by people from all over Asia who respected my culture. Minnesota was where I started to open up, join activities, and build supportive friendships that helped me grow.

I went to Humboldt Senior High School in Minnesota, and it was a place that changed my life. There, I found people who respected my culture and made me feel like I belonged. It was at Humboldt that I joined activities like JROTC and College Possible, which became like families to me and helped me grow as a person.

Joining the Junior Reserve Officers' Training Corps, or JROTC, was one of the best decisions I made in high school. It taught me leadership skills and how to push myself beyond my limits. Through JROTC, I learned to be competitive in my education, set goals, and overcome fears like talking to people different from me.

College Possible was like a family to me during my high school years. They supported me and helped guide me on my path to college. Thanks to their encouragement, I was able to achieve my dream of getting accepted to the University of Minnesota.

Getting accepted to the University of Minnesota was a dream come true for me. I still remember the day I received the acceptance letter at home—it felt unbelievable after all the struggles I faced with language and fitting in. This acceptance marked a new chapter in my life where I embraced who I am and continued to work hard toward my goals.
